Project Background"},{"tag":"text","text":"
Project Ascend is a three-month program that provides structured operational support for sellers with strong supply chains but limited operating experience. The program helps sellers build sustainable, scalable growth frameworks from the ground up and supports them in achieving key GMV milestones.

"},{"tag":"text","text":"2. Project Objectives"},{"tag":"text","text":"
Leveraging platform support and operational strategies, we aim to help merchants achieve $65K GMV in a 30-day period at least once during the program cycle.

"},{"tag":"text","text":"3. Project overview"},{"tag":"text","text":"
- Project Operation Period: 2026-02-01 to 2026-04-30
- Latest Signing Date: 2026-02-15
- Quota Limit: Total 200 spots open, first-come-first-served principle, final spot confirmation based on completed payment signing

"},{"tag":"text","text":"4. Project Highlights"},{"tag":"text","text":"
- Creator Content: Precise mid-tier+ creator matching, delivering "},{"tag":"text","text":"300 creator short videos"},{"tag":"text","text":" per quarter.
- Seller Content: Generate  "},{"tag":"text","text":"150 AIGC content pieces "},{"tag":"text","text":" per month.
- Marketing Support: Support through search and recommendation, and special Shop Tab resource positions and targeted activity tags.
- Platform Subsidies: Dedicated marketing support, fast-track channels and platform product subsidies.
- Governance & Compliance Protection: Shop health monitoring & assistance, and shop operation troubleshooting.
- Operational Support: Product diagnosis & optimization, and insights to support performance improvement.

"},{"tag":"text","text":"5. Co-investment Plans and Seller Requirements"},{"tag":"img","image_key":"img_v3_02uj_b834dd5b-afa6-4ee6-a450-66e1eb5bc37h"},{"tag":"hr"},{"tag":"text","text":"For additional details, please refer to the project one-pager and contact your Account Manager."},{"tag":"text","text":"

"},{"tag":"text","text":"You're also welcome to click the button below to register for our webinar and get your questions answered live by the project team."},{"tag":"button","text":"26'Q1 - US Project Ascend Onepager","type":"primary"},{"tag":"button","text":"Click to join TikTok Shop Webinar","type":"primary"}]]}

Do not use AI to mislead, deceive, or create false impressions about products, people, or facts.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Disclose AI Use Clearly: If your content is fully or significantly created/altered by AI, you should label it. You can do this by adding a text note/watermark in your video or by using the platform's \"AI-generated content\" toggle in the posting settings.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Do Not Impersonate Others: Do not use AI to imitate or replicate another person’s identity, likeness, or voice to create false endorsements or promotions.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Show Products Accurately: Do not use AI to alter a product’s appearance (such as size, color, or features) or to fabricate unrealistic or otherwise misleading results that are difficult to achieve in real life. The product shown must accurately match the product being sold.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Respect Intellectual Property: Do not use AI to create content that uses someone else's likeness, voice, name, or trademarks without proper authorization.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Understand the Consequences: Violating these rules can lead to actions against your content (like removal or restricted visibility) and your account (like warnings, restrictions, or even permanent bans for severe violations).","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"Seller Academy Link: ","style":[]},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university/essay?identity=1&role=2&knowledge_id=491489038501663&from=policy","text":"AI-Generated Content Policy","style":[]}]]}

{"title":"🎉  US Policy Pulse January 2026","elements":[[{"tag":"img","image_key":"img_v3_02uq_69cb541b-21bb-4ae0-8760-52b5ba1f8c5h"},{"tag":"text","text":"This message is approved for external sharing. Account managers may forward this communication to sellers and creators as needed."}],[{"tag":"img","image_key":"img_v3_02uq_e8b70b8b-423d-47db-84ed-e2ef8342d23h"}],[{"tag":"hr"}],[{"tag":"text","text":"🛍️Seller Policies🛍️"}],[{"tag":"text","text":"🚚 Fulfillment Updates: What to Know Going Into 2026"},{"tag":"text","text":"
Several fulfillment updates were made in January to clarify dispatch expectations, order handling, and tracking requirements.
"},{"tag":"text","text":"What changed:"},{"tag":"text","text":"
  • Orders must now be updated to ""},{"tag":"text","text":"In Transit"},{"tag":"text","text":"""},{"tag":"text","text":" "},{"tag":"text","text":"within 2 business days
  • Orders not updated to ""},{"tag":"text","text":"Awaiting Collection"},{"tag":"text","text":"" within 5 business days will be canceled automatically
  • Counterfeit tracking details and failure to fulfill orders will be subject to stricter enforcement actions
You can use fulfillment tools such as custom handling time, backorders, or pre-orders to help manage dispatch timelines when needed. For more information, refer to our "},{"tag":"text","text":"Fulfillment Policy"},{"tag":"text","text":" （"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university/essay?knowledge_id=3995852763301633#EBCE00CE","text":"L2L"},{"tag":"text","text":"/"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller.tiktokglobalshop.com/university/essay?identity=1&role=1&knowledge_id=7089769777235729&from=policy&anchor_link=EB8600D0","text":"GS"},{"tag":"text","text":"）.
Also, The"},{"tag":"text","text":" Fulfillment Analytics"},{"tag":"text","text":" page has moved within the Seller Center and is now called *Fulfillment Performance*（"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller-us.tiktok.com/health-center/fulfillment-performance","text":"L2L"},{"tag":"text","text":"/"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller.tiktokglobalshop.com/university/essay?knowledge_id=3251211076339472&from=new-and-updated&role=1&identity=1","text":"GS"},{"tag":"text","text":"）."}],[{"tag":"text","text":"🔄 More Flexibility with Partial Refunds on Returns"},{"tag":"text","text":"
You can now issue "},{"tag":"text","text":"partial refunds"},{"tag":"text","text":" for eligible returned items after inspection.
This is helpful when:
  • Items are returned damaged or heavily used
  • Parts or components are missing
  • Products cannot be resold in original condition
Partial refunds apply to buyer-fault return reasons and give sellers more control when handling non-sellable returns. Learn more in our *Order Cancellation, Return, and Refund Policy *（"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university/essay?knowledge_id=3253210454181634#EAC100D8","text":"L2L"},{"tag":"text","text":"/"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller.tiktokglobalshop.com/university/essay?identity=1&role=1&knowledge_id=4890864774694658&from=policy&anchor_link=EA7900DA","text":"GS"},{"tag":"text","text":"）"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university/essay?knowledge_id=3253210454181634#EAC100D8","text":"."}],[{"tag":"text","text":"⚙️ Seller-Configured Refunds Just Got Smarter"},{"tag":"text","text":"

text: Stick with business as usual do not stop selling because then you run the risk of killing momentum. So usually takes a couple days once FBT gets it to scan it in and add it to your inventory slot. What you’re gonna have now is your inventory number is gonna increase so you’re gonna have the inventory for the amount you have on hand and the inventory you have and fulfilled by TikTok orders will start getting paid it through TikTok once inventory is there what I would do is start weaning down the inventory that is on hand in your own warehouse cause you could edit that number in the TikTok seller Center.

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"Hi, ","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Is there a way to get TikTok Shipping pricing for large items? The prices I see only go to 10lbs. ","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Thank you, Rho","style":[]}]]}

text:
Hi Rho! The shipping calculator was discussed in partner office hours last week.

A seller using TikTok Shipping (TTS) can get pricing for items above 10 lbs by using the Shipping Calculator in Seller Center. The static rate card only shows up to 10 lbs, but the calculator can generate rates for heavier/larger shipments based on the package details and destination. If no rate is returned, it usually means TTS does not support that weight for the selected route, and the seller should contact support or use another shipping option.

Source: https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university/essay?knowledge_id=6837882170001153

text: The “Restricted Categories” warehouse status usually means TikTok’s system has flagged the seller's products, categories, or account compliance for restrictions (prohibited/high‑risk items, missing verification, or mis‑categorization). You need to (1) check policy/compliance issues, (2) review product categories and documents, and (3) verify warehouse and shipping settings. If everything is correct, escalate through Seller or Partner Support.
rawContent: {"text":"The “Restricted Categories” warehouse status usually means TikTok’s system has flagged the seller's products, categories, or account compliance for restrictions (prohibited/high‑risk items, missing verification, or mis‑categorization). You need to (1) check policy/compliance issues, (2) review product categories and documents, and (3) verify warehouse and shipping settings. If everything is correct, escalate through Seller or Partner Support."}

text:
@_user_1 To join Subscribe and Save, a brand/shop must have an account manager and meet several performance and account‑health thresholds. A minimum SPS of 3.5 is one of the key mandatory conditions, so eligibility is directly tied to this score. If SPS falls below 3.5, the shop can lose access to the program even if other metrics look fine. Overall, Subscribe and Save is designed for shops with stable operations and good customer experience.

Here's an article that may be helpful: https://bytedance.sg.larkoffice.com/docx/VDqyd0eKHo9pGyxuAdqldXR5gtf#share-JxGcdPg4pozxCSxhVxLlcRa7gFg
rawContent: {"text":"@_user_1 To join Subscribe and Save, a brand/shop must have an account manager and meet several performance and account‑health thresholds. A minimum SPS of 3.5 is one of the key mandatory conditions, so eligibility is directly tied to this score. If SPS falls below 3.5, the shop can lose access to the program even if other metrics look fine. Overall, Subscribe and Save is designed for shops with stable operations and good customer experience.\n\nHere's an article that may be helpful: https://bytedance.sg.larkoffice.com/docx/VDqyd0eKHo9pGyxuAdqldXR5gtf#share-JxGcdPg4pozxCSxhVxLlcRa7gFg"}

text: There’s “Accepting uncertainty” and holding TikTok to a standard. These are two separate things that can exist simultaneously. Sure rolling with the punches comes with the territory but this was simply too much. We need to hold TikTok accountable for how poorly they planned for this and voice the concerns and feedback of our clients. As agencies we are able to voice the opinions of multiple sellers so it is our responsibility to speak ups hen they do something messed up like this. There is clearly a huge disconnect between actual sellers and whoever is making these decisions. If accepting that every time tiktok sends out an email with a policy change it’s not 100% true is how you want to run your agency then it sounds like you have everything figured out. Personally I don’t feel this is an acceptable outcome. I want whoever is seeing these messages from TikTok to know that I want them to do better in the future and I’m sure I’m not the only one who feels this way.

text: This is expected behavior, not a bug.“Approve Creators” only restricts who can get free samples / approval-based collabs, not who can buy refundable samples. Once a creator purchases a refundable sample, they can immediately add the product to their showcase and tag videos, even if their approval request is still pending. To limit content to approved creators only, the seller must disable refundable samples for that product.
rawContent:

This is expected behavior, not a bug.“Approve Creators” only restricts who can get free samples / approval-based collabs, not who can buy refundable samples. Once a creator purchases a refundable sample, they can immediately add the product to their showcase and tag videos, even if their approval request is still pending. To limit content to approved creators only, the seller must disable refundable samples for that product.

text: @_user_1 If you’re getting a “product already linked” error or your Shopify product isn’t showing up when trying to map it to TikTok Shop, you can log into the TikTok Seller Center and search for the product by SKU under the product listings, since it may already be connected to an existing listing that needs to be deleted or unlinked first. Next, go into Shopify, open the product, and make sure TikTok is enabled under Sales Channels, because if it isn’t published to the TikTok channel it won’t appear for mapping. Then check every variant and confirm it has a unique SKU (no duplicates anywhere in your store), a price, inventory quantity, and weight entered, since missing or duplicate data will prevent it from syncing. If everything looks correct and it still won’t map, disconnect the TikTok sales channel inside Shopify, wait a few minutes, reconnect it, and re-sync the products to reset the connection. This has worked every single time for every one of my sellers accounts. This is a super common issue.
rawContent: {"text":"@_user_1 If you’re getting a “product already linked” error or your Shopify product isn’t showing up when trying to map it to TikTok Shop, you can log into the TikTok Seller Center and search for the product by SKU under the product listings, since it may already be connected to an existing listing that needs to be deleted or unlinked first. Next, go into Shopify, open the product, and make sure TikTok is enabled under Sales Channels, because if it isn’t published to the TikTok channel it won’t appear for mapping. Then check every variant and confirm it has a unique SKU (no duplicates anywhere in your store), a price, inventory quantity, and weight entered, since missing or duplicate data will prevent it from syncing. If everything looks correct and it still won’t map, disconnect the TikTok sales channel inside Shopify, wait a few minutes, reconnect it, and re-sync the products to reset the connection. This has worked every single time for every one of my sellers accounts. This is a super common issue. "}

text:
@_user_1 Hi! How are you?
Yes — actually I have several of my top creators being affected by this same violation. TikTok has removed their main accounts, and these are creators who literally live off TikTok Shop. Some of them are now having to go back to traditional jobs because their commissions are frozen and their accounts are gone.
I’m not sure if other agencies are experiencing the same issue — sudden violations that don’t make sense and frozen commissions — but from our side, this feels completely out of control. The support we’re receiving from TikTok has not been what we expected, which is very concerning.
I do have an important question as well:
Are creators no longer allowed to have more than one account? Based on the violation you’re sharing, it sounds like that might be the case — and if so, that would be even more worrying.
Does anyone have more clarity or updated information about this policy?
Any insights would really help. Thank you, and happy day everyone.

text:
Yes, you are not allowed to sell from another market technically, and the system often doesn't like it even if you have gone through all of the registration etc in the local market.  We had a shop blocked/deleted because people logged in from another market.

If all of the local T&C's have been met to open the shop I would suggest using a different chrome profile and the use of a VPN to manage things remotely from another market as otherwise you can risk a ban/violation
rawContent: {"text":"Yes, you are not allowed to sell from another market technically, and the system often doesn't like it even if you have gone through all of the registration etc in the local market.  We had a shop blocked/deleted because people logged in from another market.\n\nIf all of the local T&C's have been met to open the shop I would suggest using a different chrome profile and the use of a VPN to manage things remotely from another market as otherwise you can risk a ban/violation"}

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"Hi everyone, I have a question, we are a new TSP: Do we earn commission on every single sale a creator makes as an affiliate, or only on sales generated through specific product sets or campaigns we’ve invited them to?","style":[]}]]}

text: Morning! Welcome ☺️ If you are referring to the CAP/TAP (now called Creator Matchmaking) programs, you can attach a percentage or “fee” when linking to a creator as well as by creating TAP links and earning a commission on each sale made. Creators do not have to be linked to send TAP links to them as long as they click your link and add the product to their showcase, then you can earn a commission once an item has sold but they must use your TAP links. It makes sense to link to a creator if you wanted to have more insight into analytics and help them with account issues.
rawContent:

Morning! Welcome ☺️ If you are referring to the CAP/TAP (now called Creator Matchmaking) programs, you can attach a percentage or “fee” when linking to a creator as well as by creating TAP links and earning a commission on each sale made. Creators do not have to be linked to send TAP links to them as long as they click your link and add the product to their showcase, then you can earn a commission once an item has sold but they must use your TAP links. It makes sense to link to a creator if you wanted to have more insight into analytics and help them with account issues.

text:
GMV Max (GMV Max) ROI does not factor in creator commission or processing/transaction fees.
It is calculated as Gross revenue ÷ Ad cost, where gross revenue excludes sales tax but does not deduct creator commissions or platform/processing fees.

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"We used the combined products feature, and we had a question:","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"If the affiliate has one product tagged in the video, and that is the sample, we sent them with a set commission, but the buyer clicks into the listing and decides to buy a different product featured with the combined products feature. ","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"How does commission work in this situation?","style":[]}]]}

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"In this situation, the affiliate will still receive commission.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"The commission is attributed to that affiliate (who tagged the product) but is calculated based on the commission rate of the SKU actually purchased, not the originally tagged SKU.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Detailed Analysis","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"User action path","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Affiliate tags Product A in a video (Product A is part of a Combined Products listing).","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Buyer clicks the tag, enters the combined listing page.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Buyer instead chooses Product B from the same combined products set and checks out.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"2. Attribution logic","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"The order is attributed to the affiliate who drove the click into that combined listing.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"As long as the purchase is from a product within that same combined products group, the affiliate remains credited.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"3. Commission calculation","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"The commission rate used is the one configured for Product B (the SKU actually purchased).","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"The originally tagged Product A’s commission does not control the payout; it only serves as the entry point.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"4. Key takeaway","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Attribution follows the affiliate who brought the buyer into the combined listing.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Commission rate follows the final purchased SKU within that same combined products listing.","style":[]}]]}

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"Does anyone have information regarding how many accounts a creator is permitted to have within the TikTok Shop Affiliate Program?","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"I currently have several creators who received a violation notice stating that their accounts were closed due to having another account in the TikTok Shop Affiliate Program that was not in compliance with TikTok Shop policies. I would appreciate any clarification on this matter.","style":[]}]]}

text:
A single individual can hold up to 5 TikTok Shop Affiliate creator accounts under one government ID.
Each account must complete its own identity verification to get affiliate permissions.

TikTok enforces rules at the identity level: if one account under a given ID seriously violates TikTok Shop policies, TikTok may suspend or close other accounts linked to the same identity. So non‑compliance on any one affiliate account can put all other affiliate accounts of that creator at risk.
But the creators I’m talking about did not have another account closed also TikTok told me yesterday that we could only have 2 accounts under the same ID 😭

text:
For a customer‑feedback–driven product quality violation (not listing or Restricted Category related), you should submit:
Strong product safety/compliance evidence (test reports + certificates),
Proof of robust QC and manufacturing control,
Clear customer service handling records, and
A concise corrective & preventive action plan.
Combining objective lab/quality documents with a structured explanation of what happened and what you’ve changed gives you the best chance of a successful appeal for your top‑selling product.
rawContent:
{"text":" For a customer‑feedback–driven product quality violation (not listing or Restricted Category related), you should submit:
Strong product safety/compliance evidence (test reports + certificates),
Proof of robust QC and manufacturing control,
Clear customer service handling records, and
A concise corrective & preventive action plan.
Combining objective lab/quality documents with a structured explanation of what happened and what you’ve changed gives you the best chance of a successful appeal for your top‑selling product."}

text:
TikTok Shipping (US) only exposes Standard/Express tiers; a specific “2‑day air” option is not available as a distinct selection. To guarantee 2‑day air with chosen carriers, your client should use Seller Shipping.
For Seller Shipping, the best tracking sync is an automatic integration: either TikTok Shop API or a TikTok‑connected OMS/ERP/3PL that pushes carrier + tracking number + ship date in real time. For non‑technical setups, bulk CSV upload from their system to Seller Center is the next‑best option; manual entry is only suitable for very low volume.

text:
You can request permission through the Business Center from affiliates to enable live streaming GMV Max ads via QR code. Once the affiliate approves the request in the Business Center, you will be able to add the creator’s profile when setting up GMV Max Live Ads.

When the creator goes live, the GMV Max ads will automatically start spending.

As a best practice, it is recommended to onboard existing affiliates who are already performing well with video content for the brands you manage. Additionally, try to create exclusive discount links with extra offers to build urgency and drive more customer acquisitions through live streaming.

text:
From everything we’ve seen it massively depends on the brand and their approach. Some brands are 95% affiliate and short video, some 95% short video and some 95% brand live streams. 

Part of the framework from TikTik shop shows the algorithm works best when there is a balance of brand short video and live, affiliate/creator short video and live. 

We know that some brands who have focused just on one form of content eventually start tapping out in terms of growth and the normal advice from TikTok is to balance all content types and typically sprinkle in some promotion/advertising of each of those too. The algorithm responds best to a balanced approach.

text:
Hey Rivaldo, 

1. Regarding Q4 incentives, we received your invoice but it takes around 45 days for the payment to be fully completed.
2. Regarding 1:1 management, partners above the V3 tier might be eligible to have a SPM. But please keep in mind that this will not necessarily unlock an SPM automatically.

The GMV is a combination of: Creator Service Live GMV + Seller Live & Short Video GMV + Target Plan GMV + Creator Matchmaking SV GMV 

For more details about the tiering system, please check this doc: https://bytedance.sg.larkoffice.com/docx/KGS7dYwHyoR3Brx4rpZltTq9gGh

text:
We’re seeing internationally that more creators are getting bans based on violations and infringements of terms of service due to the content and claims made within it, especially around health/benefits, including anything that appears to “cure” or “treat” right the way through to miracle cures or the “best product ever” 

The landscape is changing and platforms are under more pressure to police what is being said and what is allowable. I know it’s not the US but Vietnam just passed a law having platforms retain all video including live for three years so the regulators could go back and retroactively prosecute people selling and misleading consumers. 

I’m not saying that is what you were doing, but the amount of creators who are “losing their livelihood” but who have never read the actual rules is pretty shocking…

From my experience, TikTok mostly bans first and asks questions later. 

Whilst this is more focused on live, a lot of the rules are the same for short video: https://stickler.live/2026/03/11/understanding-advertising-rules-for-live-selling/

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"Hi Partner Accelerator Team,","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"My partner currently operates two TikTok Shop accounts under the same LLC. Each account has its own separate FBT warehouse/account. We would like to consolidate and link both shops to a single FBT warehouse. Could you please advise on how to deactivate or remove one of the existing FBT accounts so we can proceed with linking them together?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Thank you for your support.","style":[]}]]}

text: You cannot currently “deactivate” or unbind an existing FBT warehouse account from a shop once it has been linked. Multiple shops under the same LLC can share a single FBT warehouse, but the change must be done by binding the second shop to the chosen FBT warehouse, not by removing the existing one. Please work with the seller’s Account Manager to review your case and plan the consolidation. If the seller does not have an AM, you’ll need to inquire with FBT using the form we’ve shared in this group.
rawContent: {"text":"You cannot currently “deactivate” or unbind an existing FBT warehouse account from a shop once it has been linked. Multiple shops under the same LLC can share a single FBT warehouse, but the change must be done by binding the second shop to the chosen FBT warehouse, not by removing the existing one. Please work with the seller’s Account Manager to review your case and plan the consolidation. If the seller does not have an AM, you’ll need to inquire with FBT using the form we’ve shared in this group."}

text: When a SKU is in multiple TikTok Shop promotions, the system uses a “best price within each layer + fixed layer priority” logic. Within the platform campaign layer (Deal Discovery, Daily & Weekly Deals, Spring Glow Up), discounts do not stack: the system picks a single effective campaign price based on campaign rules/priority and qualification. Seller promos (seller discounts, vouchers, bundles) then apply on top according to their own layer rules. When Spring Glow Up starts and the SKU is enrolled, the campaign price usually overrides evergreen platform deals if its price meets the campaign’s required thresholds; shoppers will see the Spring campaign price, strikethrough from the valid reference price, and a Spring campaign badge.
rawContent: {"text":"When a SKU is in multiple TikTok Shop promotions, the system uses a “best price within each layer + fixed layer priority” logic. Within the platform campaign layer (Deal Discovery, Daily & Weekly Deals, Spring Glow Up), discounts do not stack: the system picks a single effective campaign price based on campaign rules/priority and qualification. Seller promos (seller discounts, vouchers, bundles) then apply on top according to their own layer rules. When Spring Glow Up starts and the SKU is enrolled, the campaign price usually overrides evergreen platform deals if its price meets the campaign’s required thresholds; shoppers will see the Spring campaign price, strikethrough from the valid reference price, and a Spring campaign badge."}

A couple of technical questions from clients when onboarding to TTS:","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Can a client with global tt account link with US TTS?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Does the username of TT account have to match the shop name in seller center for two accounts to link?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"When linking shop accounts with a newly created official account instead of existing official account, where can the client sign in into the newly created official account?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"It would be very appreciated if anyone could help with these!","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Best,","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Kexin","style":[]}]]}

text: Yes it's in addition to the standard referral fees. You also do not have all of your products included in it. This is not an ad program in the sense of something like gmv max. The sellers are not actually spending any money up front like they do for gmv Max and do not create ads through this program. The fee for this program is just deducted from the orders themselves. The $1 to $5 is not a match. It's just stating that for every $1 that a seller is covering from an order that they should expect at least $5 in return. It's just used to provide you a general Roi minimum. For example most of my sellers since last year have averaged between a 16 and 20 roi. So basically as an example, one of the brands would make $100,000 in a month that came from this program and their cost that was deducted from the orders added up to about $5000

text: Yes the fee is an additional fee. Sellers do not get any money paid out like that. They're not putting up money up front like they would for a GMV max. The one to five is just simply stating that there's going to be least a 5x return on the amount of cost the brand covers through the fee deduction for the program per order

text:
Sellers are not allowed to use any TikTok logos or other trademarks in their online or offline marketing materials, including graphic images in product listings, unless explicit permission is granted by TikTok.

For the official “Available on TikTok Shop” logo, sellers may use it only if they are onboarded and actively selling, and only for advertising that their products are available on TikTok Shop. The logo must not be altered, co-branded, or used on physical packaging or on the product itself.
rawContent:
{"text":"

Sellers are not allowed to use any TikTok logos or other trademarks in their online or offline marketing materials, including graphic images in product listings, unless explicit permission is granted by TikTok.

For the official “Available on TikTok Shop” logo, sellers may use it only if they are onboarded and actively selling, and only for advertising that their products are available on TikTok Shop. The logo must not be altered, co-branded, or used on physical packaging or on the product itself.

"}

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"Hi ","style":[]},{"tag":"at","user_id":"@_user_1","user_name":"Kexin Bian","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":", please see responses to your questions below:","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"A global TikTok account can generally be linked to a US TikTok Shop, but the seller itself must meet US-market onboarding requirements (US-eligible entity/individual, address, tax/ID, etc.).","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"The TikTok account username does not need to be identical to the Seller Center shop name for binding.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"A “newly created official shop account” behaves as a normal TikTok account: it is used and signed in via the regular TikTok app / web login; current materials do not describe any extra or special login entry created by Seller Center.","style":[]}]]}

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"Hi ","style":[]},{"tag":"at","user_id":"@_user_1","user_name":"Cel","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":", please see information regarding your question below:","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"TikTok Shop campaigns and partner/agency workflows ","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"are tied to the market where the shop operates.","style":["bold"]},{"tag":"text","text":" For the US market, TikTok Shop policy notes that if TikTok Shop does not operate in your country and you want to sell in the US market, ","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"the merchant must meet US-based requirements such as U.S. citizenship/legal residency for individuals or U.S. business registration with a domestic warehouse for business applicants.","style":["bold"]},{"tag":"text","text":"For partner/agency campaign participation, the retrieved materials show that sellers can work with partners/agencies through TikTok Shop campaign tools, and you should be able to run a TAP campaign with a non-US TikTok Shop for example a POP (overseas) seller.","style":[]}]]}

To help resolve your question, please follow these steps:

1️⃣ Review the [TSP Post-Onboarding Guide](https://bytedance.us.larkoffice.com/docx/PG5Cd0nDkoiSpFxAGk8ucNqKsAg)
2️⃣ Check the [TikTok Shop US Academy](https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university) for relevant help articles and training materials
3️⃣ If the issue relates to a bound seller or creator and isn’t resolved above, please submit a [Partner Center ticket](https://partner.tiktokshop.com/docv2/page/6661ee49ea792702c925ff02)

If you experience issues with your Partner Center ticket, please share screenshots or a screen recording of the full ticket thread below so we can assist further.

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"I've come across this with one of my clients. But in my clients scenario, they outsourced this request because the vendor was not in US. It ended up triggering a shop deactivation. ","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"The process I did was: ","style":[]}],[{"tag":"text","text":"Reach out to Seller Center Customer Service. Get a ticket ID created. Share what happened. Ask them specifically what is needed for the appeal. (Request sample docs) ","style":[]}],[{"tag":"text","text":"Ask seller to provide all requested documentation/information","style":[]}],[{"tag":"text","text":"appeal with those docs ","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"If appeal is unsuccessful (I had 2 chances to appeal for this event) , I escalated it to Partner Center customer service and requested further info on why it was rejected and for them to submit the requested documentation. They always have insight as to why it was rejected - more than the vague reason we receive from the compliance team. ","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"I've had some silly rejection reasons like \"pic looks blurry\" or specific words were not accepted in certain documentation. So requesting sample docs was a key best practice I learned through this tumultuous event.","style":[]}]]}

text:
Hi @_user_1, 

To help resolve your question, please follow these steps:

1️⃣ Review the [TSP Post-Onboarding Guide](https://bytedance.us.larkoffice.com/docx/PG5Cd0nDkoiSpFxAGk8ucNqKsAg)
2️⃣ Check the [TikTok Shop US Academy](https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university) for relevant help articles and training materials
3️⃣ If the issue relates to a bound seller or creator and isn’t resolved above, please submit a [Partner Center ticket](https://partner.tiktokshop.com/docv2/page/6661ee49ea792702c925ff02)

If you experience issues with your Partner Center ticket, please share screenshots or a screen recording of the full ticket thread below so we can assist further.

Additionally, if the creator has a creator manager, I highly encourage you to have her ask her manager directly.

text: Yes the ROI guarantee is still in effect. This has been in effect since June of 2025. You do not have to be enrolled in the program. Tiktok also has stated that sellers can still utilize the standard promotional tools since it's separate from the smart promotion program as that program was formerly known as the co-funded marketing program. But keep in mind moving forward, a lot of platform-based campaigns require you to be enrolled in the smart promotion program.

Tiktok also has stated that sellers can still utilize the standard promotional tools since it's separate from the smart promotion program as that program was formerly known as the co-funded marketing program. {"title":"Earn 10% FBT fulfillment Fee Back!🔥🔥🔥","card_link":{"url":"","android_url":"","ios_url":"","pc_url":""},"elements":[[{"tag":"img","image_key":"img_v3_0210j_69c70003-bbdc-4c94-af4c-d62fd0d21c0h"},{"tag":"text","text":"Dear TikTok Shop seller,

TikTok Shop is excited to announce the 2026 April to September FBT Incremental Units Incentive Program! This program rewards sellers who increase their Fulfilled by TikTok (FBT) units in any given month compared to February 2026.

Sellers who deliver "},{"tag":"text","text":"600 or more incremental FBT units in any calendar month above their February 2026 units"},{"tag":"text","text":" from April through September 2026 "},{"tag":"text","text":"will be eligible to receive a 10% reimbursement of FBT fulfillment fees for those incremental units"},{"tag":"text","text":", capped at $1.00 per unit.

Each month is calculated separately, so there is no cross-month carryover. Participation is subject to the terms and conditions of the Incentive Program.

To participate, make sure your shop has completed FBT onboarding and register for the program in Seller Center:"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university/essay?knowledge_id=8644984183162670&default_language=en#how-to-onboard-fbt-join-now","text":"👉👉FBT onboarding and register"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university/essay?knowledge_id=762581078214413","text":"👉👉Terms and conditions"},{"tag":"hr"},{"tag":"text","text":"1. Go to "},{"tag":"text","text":"Growth → Growth Opportunities → For You Tab."},{"tag":"text","text":"
2. Find the mission** “Registration for the 2026 April to September FBT Incentive Program.”**
3. Click the "},{"tag":"text","text":"mission banner."},{"tag":"text","text":"
4. Enter the mission page and "},{"tag":"text","text":"read the incentive program instructions."},{"tag":"text","text":"
5. Click "},{"tag":"text","text":"Register."},{"tag":"text","text":"
For full details, including eligibility, examples, and settlement information, please check our feature guide.

If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to reach out to our seller service team via chat or by submitting a ticket."},{"tag":"a","href":"https://seller-us.tiktok.com/university/essay?knowledge_id=3267224767891214","text":"👉👉Feature guide"},{"tag":"a","href":"https://scm-us.tiktok.com/merchant-university/course-details/26919208710","text":"👉👉Submitting a ticket "},{"tag":"hr"},{"tag":"button","text":"Learn More","type":"primary"}]]}

Save time and streamline seller's participation in campaigns on TikTok Shop! Instead of registering for separate individual sub-campaigns, you can now register using two main enrollment types: "},{"tag":"text","text":"Standard Registration"},{"tag":"text","text":" and "},{"tag":"text","text":"Advanced Registration"},{"tag":"text","text":"
- If eligible, you can "},{"tag":"text","text":"register with both types"},{"tag":"text","text":". It's recommended to prioritize enrollment through Advanced registration first to unlock even more benefits.
- Premium Offer and Flash Sale are "},{"tag":"text","text":"combined under"},{"tag":"text","text":" Advanced Registration, benefits will take effect based on which tactic sellers are invited.
- "},{"tag":"text","text":"Campaign inventory is no longer locked"},{"tag":"text","text":", and all of your campaigns will share the same stock. Sellers can set a stock limit: The maximum number of units you allow to sell at your registered campaign price. This will not impact your available stock in other campaigns.

Starting April, the Smart Promotion (SP) / Smart Promotion (co-funded) is a requirement for campaign registration and participation in the campaign.
- If you are in the SP, this program will charge a Fee of "},{"tag":"text","text":"4.5%"},{"tag":"text","text":" of seller's entire shop GMV on an order level during the campaign period, and 3.5% of the GMV during the non-campaign period.
- If you are in the Smart Promotion (co-funded), during campaign periods, co-funding-based charges remain unchanged, with an additional 1% service fee applied to the entire store's GMV.
- To learn more about the benefits and fees, please reach out to your Account Manager for more information.

"},{"tag":"text","text":"Benefits"},{"tag":"text","text":": The SP/CFP program will allocate seller's promotional spending to improve subsidy efficiency, product conversion, and overall GMV performance.

- Premium Exposure: Coupons and promotions are surfaced throughout page highlights, and other high-exposure placements.
"},{"tag":"text","text":"* "},{"tag":"text","text":"Actual results may vary. The 70% GMV uplift is based on internal TikTok Shop data comparing participating versus non-participating sellers during the specific period"},{"tag":"text","text":"

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"TT shop violation - appeals failed","style":["bold"]},{"tag":"text","text":"Hi everyone","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Does anyone know what can be done next in order to regain the normal status of a TT Shop if 2 appeals failed?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"We've received the status \"Additional Information Needed To Verify Your Identity And Business\" and provided all the needed info including relative videos, photos, contracts, invoices, etc.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Upon providing all the necessary info, we were informed to provide an updated version of invoice so that the description of the product matches with the description of the listing in the TT Shop. Having corrected the invoice and uploaded it again along with the rest of the documents, now we are facing the second appeal being denied for the reason \"Your purchase contract or invoice is blurry, pixelated, illegible, cropped, or incomplete. Upload a complete, clear, readable, and high-resolution image of the document\", which makes absolutely no sense as the invoice is clear, visible, has the name of the registered company and the description of the product matches with the one in the listing.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"The support team keeps referring us to the Seller Enforcement Policy, yet it is not clear what can be done next with our client's shop","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"If anyone has recently had  a similar issue, we would extremely appreciate your help or advice on this matter as we are at our wits' end of what can or should be done next.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Appreciate your time and any help regarding the above written issue ","style":[]},{"tag":"emotion","emoji_type":"Lark_Emoji_Thanks_0"}]]}

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"Any GMV Max experts in here? I just want to understand the reporting dashboards better.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Sharing some screenshots of actual dashboards from one of our clients to help illustrate (sensitive info removed). These are all from March 1 - 31:","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Seller Center Analytics","style":["bold"]},{"tag":"img","image_key":"img_v3_0210o_c758466b-5b4f-4750-bb89-d4f2262a1bhu","width":1518,"height":1231},{"tag":"text","text":"GMV Max Campaign Dashboard","style":["bold"]},{"tag":"img","image_key":"img_v3_0210o_c2d677c0-6d2d-4898-8637-2d8c9645c3hu","width":1232,"height":813},{"tag":"text","text":"GMV Max Creatives Dashboard","style":["bold"]},{"tag":"img","image_key":"img_v3_0210o_b57106b4-304d-46f5-8a9b-4efe3f8c4fhu","width":1220,"height":774},{"tag":"text","text":"Seller Center reports we earned $16,743.23 GMV in March. Okay, cool.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"The GMV Max Campaign dashboard says that \"Gross Revenue\" for March was $15,055.16. According to the tooltip, \"Gross Revenue\" is \"The total gross revenue of Shop orders attributed to your campaign. It's the amount the user pays, plus all Shop price subsidies provided to the user.\" It's a little suspect that almost 90% of this shop's total GMV for March could be attributed to GMV Max, but if that's true, fine.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"However, the GMV Max Creatives Dashboard says that our \"Gross Revenue\" was $7,444.63 USD. This seems more in line with expectations (about 44% attribution to GMV Max), but then why is there such a large delta between what the campaign dashboard and creatives dashboard say?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Additionally, the first screenshot shows Product Card GMV at $8,207.50. The tooltip for \"Product Card GMV\" provides this example:","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"A customer searches for a product in the Shop tab or browses a seller's shop, then clicks the product card and places an order directly. This counts as product card GMV. If they clicked on any creator or seller shoppable content and purchased the product, it won't be counted toward product card GMV.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Given that GMV Max's whole purpose is to use ","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"shoppable content","style":["italic"]},{"tag":"text","text":" to generate sales, \"Product Card GMV\" should be completely separate from GMV Max and none of its GMV should be attributable to a GMV Max campaign. The example quoted above reinforces that.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"So I'm more inclined to believe the Gross Revenue as reported by the Creatives Dashboard is everything that could be attributed to a GMV Max Campaign. I don't know why the number on the Campaign Dashboard is more than double that.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Ultimately, my questions are:","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"What's the best way to find out how much GMV in a given period could be directly attributed to GMV Max campaigns?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"If it's by looking at the gross revenue reported by GMV Max ","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Campaign Dashboard","style":["bold"]},{"tag":"text","text":", how can the discrepancy between that number ($15,055.16) and the amount reported in \"Product Card GMV\" ($8,207.50) be explained?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"If it's by looking at the gross revenue reported by GMV Max ","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Creatives Dashboard","style":["bold"]},{"tag":"text","text":" ($7,444.63), what is causing the campaign dashboard to report more than double that number? Or another way to put it: what else is GMV Max spending on if not shoppable videos/lives?","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Again, the screenshots and numbers are all from March 1 - 31. However, the discrepancies/questions remain unclear even when tracking back to previous months. None of the numbers just seem to line up.","style":[]},{"tag":"text","text":"Thanks for any advice!","style":[]}]]}

text: Whenever you look at the GMV Max dashboard, GMV Max takes ownership and attributes sales for the entire platform. That means if a sale comes from an affiliate organically on TikTok, or from the product card, GMV Max is still going to take ownership of that and attribute it to the overall ad spend and ROI. There's no visibility into what came from organic or GMV Max. It's just the way TikTok shop has it set up.
rawContent: {"text":"Whenever you look at the GMV Max dashboard, GMV Max takes ownership and attributes sales for the entire platform. That means if a sale comes from an affiliate organically on TikTok, or from the product card, GMV Max is still going to take ownership of that and attribute it to the overall ad spend and ROI. There's no visibility into what came from organic or GMV Max. It's just the way TikTok shop has it set up."}

{"title":"","content":[[{"tag":"text","text":"Dear Partners,","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"We are seeking your professional advice regarding a GMV Max strategy for one of our current projects. At present, we are running a single campaign that includes all products with revenue exceeding $25k. However, we have observed that 90% of the advertising budget is being allocated to one primary product, preventing the others from receiving sufficient exposure to scale.","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"To address this, we are planning to transition the main product and other high-potential items into dedicated individual campaigns to facilitate further expansion. While this seems necessary for growth, we are concerned about the associated risks, specifically the potential for a temporary decline in sales during the algorithm's re-learning phase.","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"We would be very grateful if anyone could share their experience with a similar transition. Specifically, we are interested in whether this fragmentation improved overall performance in the long term or if you encountered significant stability issues.","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"Additionally, we have been unable to establish contact with our Account Manager, Allison Li. If anyone could assist us in reaching her or provide guidance on the best way to get a response, it would be greatly appreciated.","style":[]}],[],[{"tag":"text","text":"Thank you in advance for your insights and assistance.","style":[]}]]}

text: Yes, You will see a dip. That's normal and expected. When you launch a new campaign it starts from scratch, no historical data, no creative learnings, no audience signals. The algorithm needs time to figure out what works.
rawContent: {"text":"Yes, You will see a dip. That's normal and expected. When you launch a new campaign it starts from scratch, no historical data, no creative learnings, no audience signals. The algorithm needs time to figure out what works."}

text: I have not had a good experience with fbt at all. text:
If you mean AI hosts, we have done quite a bit in this space in other markets and the short answer is that they don't work, or we've never seen them work (if you mean AI assitance in running live streams then drop me a note, it is what we do).

In China the AI hosts are basically banned on Douyin.  They get some press on other platforms but they account for large percentages of live volume and very few sales. On TikTok Shop in Southeast Asia they are "allowed" but they basically get algorithmic shadow bans pretty quickly because of the lack of interactivity.  Consumers stop and check them out, and either scroll past or have a brief play with them to see if they are interactive, and then they move on.  This means that pretty quickly the algorithm shows them as a boring and non-responsive live stream and the live gets downranked to the point it gets "banned"

A couple of posts I wrote on this over the last little while: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/fionnhyndman_ai-livecommerce-tiktok-activity-7383426014380564480-hdKb

and one from a while back that still holds:  https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/ai-hosts-droids-you-looking-fionn-hyndman-iwece/
